county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. Young county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Ward 1910. NW ND — Canadian border (Saskatchewan). Named for John Burke, ND governor. Bowbells is county seat. Norwegian and Scandinavian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Burke County Recorder (Bowbells). Formed 1910 — one of ND's youngest counties. Northwestern corner near Saskatchewan border. Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish) settlement predominant. Pre-1910 records under Ward County. Limited local repositories; SHSND is primary archive. Check Norwegian Lutheran church records. Border families may appear in Saskatchewan provincial records. Burke County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.

Norwegian immigrants.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. Excellent preservation — state capital.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created and organized 1873. BISMARCK is the state capital and county seat. Named for Walter Burleigh, territorial delegate. Missouri River. SHSND physically located here. Fort Abraham Lincoln (Custer's departure point for Little Bighorn 1876) nearby.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Burleigh County Recorder (Bismarck — state capital). SHSND located in Bismarck is THE central ND genealogy repository — start here for any ND research. Germans from Russia Heritage Society (GRHS) headquarters in Bismarck — essential for German-Russian families statewide. Fort Abraham Lincoln State Park (Custer's 7th Cavalry post) — military records at NARA. Bismarck Public Library genealogy collection. Excellent early records from 1873. State vital records office also in Bismarck.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Germans from Russia, Norwegian, and diverse immigrants.

source_profiles.source_name: State Historical Society of North Dakota — Central Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: THE definitive ND genealogy repository. Territorial records, state records, newspapers, manuscripts, Germans from Russia collections. 612 E Boulevard Ave, Bismarck.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. Excellent preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created and organized 1873. FARGO is county seat — ND's largest city. North Dakota State University. Named for George Washington Cass, railroad president. Red River Valley. Fargo-Moorhead metro area (Moorhead is in MN Clay County).
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Cass County Recorder (Fargo — ND's largest city). Excellent online records portal. NDSU Archives and Institute for Regional Studies hold Bonanza farm records, Red River Valley land company records, and extensive photograph collections. Fargo Public Library has genealogy section. Red River Valley — Norwegian, German, and Scandinavian settlement. City directories for Fargo from 1881. Cass County Historical Society at Bonanzaville. SHSND microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Norwegian, German, and diverse immigrants.
Outstanding regional genealogy collections. Immigration records, family papers, North Dakota biography collection, Norwegian-American materials.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873, organized 1884. NE corner of ND — Canadian border (Manitoba). Named for Charles Cavalier, customs inspector. Langdon is county seat. Icelandic settlement significant. Pembina Trail.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Cavalier County Recorder (Langdon). Border county with Manitoba. Significant Icelandic settlement (Mountain/Akra area) — Icelandic collections at UND and Chester Fritz Library. Check Manitoba Provincial Archives for cross-border Icelandic families. Cavalier County Historical Society. Also Polish and Ukrainian settlement in western portions — check Catholic church records. SHSND microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Icelandic and Norwegian immigrants.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1881, organized 1882. SE ND. Named for George Dickey, territorial legislator. Ellendale is county seat. German and Scandinavian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Dickey County Recorder (Ellendale). German-Russian settlement significant in western portion; Scandinavian in eastern. Ellendale was briefly ND's territorial capital and home to State Normal School (teacher training — school records at SHSND). Dickey County Historical Society in Ellendale. Check Lutheran and German Evangelical church records. SHSND microfilm.

Statewide index. Germans from Russia and Scandinavian immigrants.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. Young county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Williams 1910. NW corner of ND — Canadian border (Saskatchewan) and MT border. Named for the divide between the Missouri and Hudson Bay watersheds. Crosby is county seat. Scandinavian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Divide County Recorder (Crosby). Formed 1910 — one of ND's last counties organized. Northwestern corner bordering Montana and Saskatchewan. Pre-1910 records under Williams County. Scandinavian settlement predominant. Crosby area settled during last homestead rush 1905-1915. Limited local repositories; SHSND primary archive. Border families may appear in Montana or Saskatchewan records. Divide County Historical Society.
